Metric: Software name? (string)
URL? (URL)
Affiliation (institution(s)) (string or N/A)
Response: Meta AI Research (FAIR — Fundamental AI Research); developed by Facebook AI Research; AI Habitat project part of FAIR's embodied AI research program led by researchers including Erik Wijmans, Abhishek Kadian, Amir Sadeghi, Claudia Perez-D'Arpino, and many others

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Flexible, high-performance 3D simulator for embodied AI research; enables training and evaluation of embodied AI agents (virtual robots, navigation agents) in photorealistic 3D environments; provides efficient 3D scene rendering (Magnum renderer), dataset support (Matterport3D, Gibson, Replica, HM3D), and physics simulation via Bullet; companion to Habitat-Lab RL training framework

Last commit date? (date)
Status? (alive is defined as presence of commits in the last 18 months) (alive, dead, unclear)
License? (GNU GPL, BSD, MIT, terms of use, trial, none, unclear, other∗) ∗ given via a string
Platforms? (set of Windows, Linux, OS X, Android, other∗) ∗ given via string

Software Category? The concept category includes software that does not have an officially released version. Public software has a released version in the public domain. Private software has a released version available to authorized users only. (concept, public, private)
Development model? (open source, freeware, commercial, unclear)
Publications about the software? Refers to publications that have used or mentioned the software. (number or unknown)
Source code URL? (set of url, n/a, unclear)
Programming language(s)? (set of FORTRAN, Matlab, C, C++, Java, R, Ruby, Python, Cython, BASIC, Pascal, IDL, unclear, other∗) ∗ given via string
Is there evidence that performance was considered? Performance refers to either speed, storage, or throughput. (yes∗, no)
Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Habitat-Sim is designed specifically for embodied AI research at scale — enabling massive RL training with thousands of parallel simulation instances. Supports articulated object simulation (v0.2+), rearrangement tasks (v0.3), and multi-agent scenarios. The Habitat-Matterport 3D Dataset (HM3D) released alongside provides 1000 photorealistic indoor environments. Not primarily a robot dynamics simulator but widely used in embodied agent research. Companion to Habitat-Lab (reinforcement learning training framework).

Metric: Additional comments? (can cover any metrics you feel are missing, or any other thoughts you have)
Response: Conda install is very simple (4 steps). Docker option further simplifies GPU environment setup. Excellent documentation including Docker-based quickstart, Colab notebooks, and conda install instructions. Some 3D datasets require separate download (multi-GB).

Response: Rendering correctness validated through visual benchmarks and comparison with real images. Physics correctness less critical (RL training task) compared to precision simulators. Strong FAIR engineering practices.

Response: Reliable for embodied AI research workloads. GPU memory management for large-scale multi-process training is well-handled. Tutorial notebooks run cleanly.
